mnemonics.title: Outcode Bit Order
mnemonics.content: TBRL - Top Bottom Right Left (Think 'Tasty Biryani Rice Leftovers' or 'Tropical Beach Resort Lagoon'). Remember bits are set if OUTSIDE: 1000=Top, 0100=Bottom, 0010=Right, 0001=Left.

mnemonics.title: Algorithm Flow
mnemonics.content: OR (accept) → AND (reject) → CLIP. Remember: 'OR' sounds like 'All Right' (accept), 'AND' sounds like 'ANDroid rejecting' - think of your phone rejecting calls!

mnemonics.title: Intersection Order
mnemonics.content: Clip to boundary corresponding to first non-zero bit in outcode (reading left to right: Top→Bottom→Right→Left). Think: 'The TOp BRight Rookie Learns'.

acronyms.expansion: Outcode Rejection check (trivially accept if either = 0000)
acronyms.usage: Step 2 of algorithm - if either outcode is 0000, accept the line

acronyms.expansion: Acceptance Number Determination (trivially reject if AND ≠ 0)
acronyms.usage: Step 3 of algorithm - bitwise AND determines if line is completely outside

visualTips.visualization: Imagine the clipping window as a box. Place a point anywhere. If point is ABOVE the top edge, set top bit. If BELOW bottom, set bottom bit. Similarly RIGHT and LEFT. Picture 4 arrows pointing inward from each edge - light up arrow if point is beyond it.
When both endpoints have outcode 0000, draw a green box around the line. The entire line fits inside the window - no cutting needed!
When AND of outcodes ≠ 0, both endpoints share at least one 'outside' region. Visualize as the line passing through the same outside area - draw it in red and discard.
Line cuts through the window like a knife. The algorithm slices off the outside portion, recalculates outcode, and checks again. Repeat until fully inside or outside.

analogies.analogy: Think of outcode as a 4-digit postal code. Each digit tells you which 'neighborhood' (edge) the point is outside of. 0000 means home (inside), 1001 means out past Top AND Left avenues.
Imagine trimming a stick to fit inside a rectangular picture frame. Cohen-Sutherland finds which parts of the stick stick out and cuts exactly at the frame edges.

Two friends living in different cities both tell you they're 'outside the city limits' in the same direction - they must both be outside the same area, so the line connecting them never enters the city.

Outcode comparison is like checking if two people share the same allergy. AND checks common allergies (both allergic to same thing = reject). OR checks if either can enter (at least one has '0000' = accept).
